description:
## Smell

Primitive Obsession / Mysterious Name (Fowler, *Refactoring*). The WebDriver
harness passes scenario, phase, fixture paths, and store paths between three
processes through raw string env vars whose meaning is implicit at every
call site.

## Background

`bsm-kia.5` introduced a multi-process scenario harness. Three independent
places now read the same environment contract:

- `e2e/issue-list/scripts/run-scenario.ts:73-75` declares the `Scenario` union
  and `isScenario` type guard, and parses the CLI argument.
- `wdio.issue-list.conf.ts:33-49` redeclares the `Scenario` mapping inline
  (`rawScenario`, chained `if/else if`), reads `BEADSMITH_E2E_WORKSPACE_A`,
  `BEADSMITH_E2E_WORKSPACE_B`, `BEADSMITH_E2E_WORKSPACE_B_SECOND`, and
  `BEADSMITH_WORKSPACE_STORE_PATH` as `string | undefined`, and validates
  each one with its own ad-hoc error string.
- `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.restoration.spec.ts` and
  `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.atomic-switch.spec.ts` each read
  `process.env.BEADSMITH_E2E_PHASE ?? "1"` and branch on
  `phase === "1" | "2"` inline inside every `it()`.

There is no single function that produces the typed
`{ scenario, phase, fixtureA, fixtureB?, fixtureBSecond?, storePath }`
shape the specs actually need. Env-var names are duplicated strings, the
union lives in two files, and the type guard is implemented twice with
subtly different signatures.

## What to build

Introduce one helper that owns the harness input contract end to end:

- A single typed `Scenario` / `Phase` union and a single
  `parseScenario(input: string): Scenario` / `parsePhase(input: string): Phase`
  pair, exported from `e2e/issue-list/scripts/run-scenario.ts` (which is
  the authoritative launcher).
- A `parseHarnessEnvironment(env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv): HarnessInputs`
  function that returns a fully validated
  `{ scenario, phase, fixtureA, fixtureB?, fixtureBSecond?, storePath }`
  object, or throws one consolidated error naming every missing input
  instead of one-per-`if`.
- `wdio.issue-list.conf.ts` and each multi-phase spec import the same
  parser instead of re-reading env vars.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] One source of truth for the `Scenario` and `Phase` unions lives in
      `e2e/issue-list/scripts/run-scenario.ts` (or a small sibling module).
- [ ] `wdio.issue-list.conf.ts` and every spec read typed harness inputs
      through a shared parser rather than `process.env.*` lookups.
- [ ] Missing or unknown env values fail with one error that lists every
      invalid input, not one error per `if`.
- [ ] `rg "process\.env\.BEADSMITH_E2E_"` returns zero hits in spec files
      and the wdio config (the launcher remains the only env producer).
- [ ] `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` and
      `pnpm e2e:issue-list:isolation` still pass.

## Out of scope

- Changing which env vars the launcher exports.
- Renaming the env vars themselves; this is about typed consumption.
- Refactoring the launcher CLI surface (e.g., switching to a real option
  parser); only the parsing of the inherited environment is in scope.
title: Replace raw env-var primitives with typed Scenario/Phase/fixture inputs
